children.name: Picathartes
children.description: A genus of two unusual, medium-sized passerines endemic to the rainforests of West and Central Africa, commonly known as rockfowl and once considered to be related to crows or starlings. They are named for their habit of nesting colonially on cave walls and rock faces, building mud nests in the manner of swallows. Their striking bare heads, long tail, and upright stance give them a prehistoric appearance.
